Title: IWS - Verify IWS device can be dynamically paired
Preconditions: 1. Open "Q-SYS Designer" software.

Title: 2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
4. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open the dynamic pairing interface in Core Manager or Designer.

Title: 2. Select the IWS device from the available devices list.
3. Initiate dynamic pairing.
4. Wait for pairing to complete.
5. Verify the device status.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS device is successfully paired to the component.

Title: 2. The device reaches "OK" state.
3. Sensor data flows from the device to the component.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify IWS device can be unpaired
Preconditions: 1. Open "Q-SYS Designer" software.

Title: 2. Design deployed to Core with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is dynamically paired and in "OK" state.
Preconditions: 1. Open the dynamic pairing interface in Core Manager or Designer.

Title: 2. Select the currently paired device.
3. Initiate unpairing/removal.
4. Confirm the action if prompted.
5. Verify the component and device status.
Preconditions: 1. The device is successfully unpaired from the component.

Title: 2. The component shows an unassigned/disconnected state.
3. Sensor data stops flowing.
4. The device is available for pairing to another component.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify behavior when two components paired to same physical device
Preconditions: 1. Open "Q-SYS Designer" software.

Title: 2. Create a design with two IWS components ("IWS-1" and "IWS-2").
3. IWS device is on the network, inventory item names configured.
4. Deploy the design to the Core.
5. One physical IWS device is available on the network.
Preconditions: 1. In Core Manager or Designer, pair "IWS-1" to the physical IWS device.

Title: 2. Verify "IWS-1" is in "OK" state.
3. Attempt to pair "IWS-2" to the same physical IWS device.
4. Observe the behavior.
Preconditions: 1. The system either prevents the duplicate pairing with a clear error, OR

Title: 2. The second pairing succeeds and the first component becomes unpaired.
3. No crash or undefined behavior occurs.
4. The final state is clearly communicated to the user.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify pairings can be swapped between two devices
Preconditions: 1. Open "Q-SYS Designer" software.

Title: 2. Create a design with two IWS components ("IWS-1" and "IWS-2").
3. Two physical IWS devices are on the network ("IWS-0123" and "IWS-abcd").
4. Deploy the design to the Core.
5. "IWS-1" is paired to "IWS-0123" and "IWS-2" is paired to "IWS-abcd".
Preconditions: 1. Verify both pairings are active and in "OK" state.

Title: 2. In Core Manager or Designer, swap the pairings in a single edit: pair "IWS-1" to "IWS-abcd" and "IWS-2" to "IWS-0123".
3. Apply the changes.
4. Wait for both devices to connect.
Preconditions: 1. "IWS-1" is now paired to "IWS-abcd" and receiving its sensor data.

Title: 2. "IWS-2" is now paired to "IWS-0123" and receiving its sensor data.
3. Both devices reach "OK" state.
4. No orphaned or conflicting pairings remain.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify pairing via device name (hostname)
Preconditions: 1. Open "Q-SYS Designer" software.

Title: 2. Create a design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network with a known hostname (e.g., "IWS-LobbyA").
4. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open the dynamic pairing interface in Core Manager or Designer.

Title: 2. Select or enter the device by its hostname ("IWS-LobbyA").
3. Initiate dynamic pairing.
4. Wait for pairing to complete.
Preconditions: 1. The device is successfully paired using its hostname.

Title: 2. The device reaches "OK" state.
3. Sensor data flows correctly.
4. If the device IP changes, the pairing remains valid via hostname resolution.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify pairing via switch port (LLDP port information)
Preconditions: 1. Open "Q-SYS Designer" software.

Title: 2. Create a design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is connected to a managed switch with LLDP enabled.
4. The switch port information is known (e.g., "Switch1/Port24").
5. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open the dynamic pairing interface in Core Manager or Designer.

Title: 2. Select or enter the device by its switch port / LLDP port information.
3. Initiate dynamic pairing.
4. Wait for pairing to complete.
Preconditions: 1. The device is successfully paired using LLDP port information.

Title: 2. The device reaches "OK" state.
3. Sensor data flows correctly.
4. If the device is replaced on the same port, the new device pairs automatically.
Preconditions: Manual
